acceptance_targets: [CH09-F001] Pass if, in the revised passage around lines 211-244, at least two explicit procedural inference steps identify Hollis before his entrance; fail if removing Hollis's entrance would break the identity chain.

[CH09-F002] Pass if each revised argument block in lines 281-303 and 349-355 contains at least one concrete consequence sentence that names what was done to bodies or people (not just billing categories); fail if stakes remain mostly abstract/legal.
[CH09-F003] Pass if the revised Hollis entrance includes at least two physical risk cues and one explicit legal constraint before he exits; fail if the beat remains primarily social banter.

[CH09-F004] Pass if, in each revised exchange, a reader can attribute lines to speaker without tags from syntax and diction alone and each of Lena/Beau/Mara gets at least one line matching their style-bible stress/register profile; fail if lines remain interchangeable.

[CH09-F005] Pass if the revised cited spans use no more than two look/eye reaction beats total and tension is still legible through alternate physical actions; fail if visual-reaction defaults remain the dominant beat type.

[FBR-02-018] Pass if the revised Beau lines in the cited spans read distinctly from Lena's syntax (higher contraction use, practical idiom, non-identical sentence rhythm) and still convey the same legal strategy; fail if both characters still sound interchangeable in formal register.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised passage around lines 211-244, at least two explicit procedural inference steps identify Hollis before his entrance; fail if removing Hollis's entrance would break the identity chain.
findings.problem: The core identity reveal (HR -> Hollis Reddick) lands as convenience timing: the suspect walks in and supplies the missing linkage at the exact moment the investigators are blocked, which weakens earned causality and investigative agency.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the reveal so the name is derived before the interruption. Target spans: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:211, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:243. Have Lena/Beau force a code-legend step and one additional document cross-check; if Hollis still enters, use him as pressure escalation rather than first proof.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each revised argument block in lines 281-303 and 349-355 contains at least one concrete consequence sentence that names what was done to bodies or people (not just billing categories); fail if stakes remain mostly abstract/legal.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: the chapter's key harm linkage is repeatedly routed through euphemistic finance language (retainers, routing, admissibility) at moments that should foreground concrete human consequence, which softens narrative impact.
findings.rewrite_direction: Inject concrete consequence where euphemism currently dominates. Target spans: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:281, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:301, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:353. Pair each legal/financial claim with one specific bodily, social, or familial effect tied to the moved remains or affected families.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised Hollis entrance includes at least two physical risk cues and one explicit legal constraint before he exits; fail if the beat remains primarily social banter.
findings.problem: Timidity in the confrontation beat: a suspected evidence mover appears in arm's reach, but scene energy slips into banter and exits without fully rendering immediate tactical danger or the explicit cost of standing down.
findings.rewrite_direction: Sharpen the risk envelope while preserving legal constraints. Target spans: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:241, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:249, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:253. Add concrete room-level threat cues (positioning, evidence protection, fear response) plus one explicit line stating why they cannot detain him yet.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in each revised exchange, a reader can attribute lines to speaker without tags from syntax and diction alone and each of Lena/Beau/Mara gets at least one line matching their style-bible stress/register profile; fail if lines remain interchangeable.
findings.problem: Idiolect separation blurs in key pressure exchanges: Lena, Beau, and Mara all lean on similar abstract policy diction, which narrows voice distinctness when conflict should make registers diverge.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate speech texture in the cited exchanges. Target spans: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:123, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:355. Give Lena a procedure-to-blunt pivot, Beau a workmanlike anecdotal idiom, and Mara a precise evidence-verb challenge with sharper interrogative rhythm.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised cited spans use no more than two look/eye reaction beats total and tension is still legible through alternate physical actions; fail if visual-reaction defaults remain the dominant beat type.
findings.problem: Recurring visual-reaction formula (looked/eyes) appears repeatedly as the default interpersonal beat, flattening motion and emotional texture once it passes two uses.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary reaction mechanics in the cited beats. Target spans: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:235, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:339, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:375. Replace at least half of look/eye reactions with non-visual actions (posture, object handling, interruption, silence with consequence).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised Beau lines in the cited spans read distinctly from Lena's syntax (higher contraction use, practical idiom, non-identical sentence rhythm) and still convey the same legal strategy; fail if both characters still sound interchangeable in formal register.
findings.problem: Dialogue register flattening: in the warrant hearing/hallway run, Beau's high-contraction, workmanlike idiolect compresses into the same formal legal cadence as Lena, weakening idiolect separation under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Beau's key lines at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3200, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3222, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3230 to restore his voice signature (contractions, tactile analogies, practical side-road framing) while preserving legal substance.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
